summary: Pre-launch checkout confidence pass on https://feature-verification-7.preview.emergentagent.com. Walked the full revenue path: Homepage -> PDP (Marbletta, /shop/collection/Marbletta) -> Add-to-Cart (qty 5 m²) -> Cart (/shop/tile-cart) -> Checkout Step 1 (Your Details) -> Step 2 (Delivery Method) -> Step 3 (Payment). Cart rendering, persistence, express-checkout buttons (Klarna/PayPal), promo code field, delivery/collect toggle, VAT-inclusive totals, and step 1 validation all pass. HOWEVER the actual card-payment path is BROKEN: on Step 3 the Stripe Card Element iframe does NOT render (only the Stripe controller + metrics outer frames are present — no cardnumber/exp/cvc iframe), so a customer cannot enter card details at all. Separately, clicking 'Pay £289.94' reverts the user to Step 2 with the error 'Please fill in your delivery address' because the address entered in Step 2 is not persisted when navigating forward to Step 3 and back — classic state-loss bug. Either bug alone blocks 100% of card revenue; both together mean zero card orders can complete right now. Also surfaced a THREE-way free-delivery-threshold inconsistency (ribbon £300 / PDP hero £299 / actual cart threshold ~£1000 inferred from the 'Spend £775.05 more for FREE delivery' banner at a £224.95 subtotal).

ui_bugs.issue: Stripe Card Element does NOT render. Only __privateStripeController and __privateStripeMetricsController outer iframes are present -- there is no card-number / exp / cvc iframe inside the Credit/Debit Cards panel even when that payment method is selected. Card customers literally have no input field. Likely a mount-order bug where <CardElement> is never mounted, or stripePromise/Elements provider is missing, or the element container is conditionally rendered only after an event that never fires.

Delivery address entered in Step 2 (Address Line 1 / City / Postcode) is not persisted when the user advances to Step 3. Clicking 'Pay' on Step 3 triggers the toast 'Please fill in your delivery address' and bounces the user back to Step 2 with all address fields EMPTY, even though the address was successfully submitted on the first pass (Step 3 briefly displayed 'Delivery to 10 Downing Street, London, SW1A 1AA' in Order Review). Any back-and-forth between payment and delivery loses the entered address.
ui_bugs.selector: [data-testid=checkout-step-2] input placeholders 123 High Street / London / TN9 1PP

ui_bugs.issue: Homepage top ribbon states 'Free delivery on orders over £300'. Product Detail Page (Marbletta) says 'FREE over £299'. Cart 'Spend £775.05 more for FREE delivery' at a £224.95 subtotal implies the ACTUAL threshold being applied is ~£1000. Three different figures visible to the same shopper on the same journey. The user flagged £300 vs £499 in TileStationHome.js fallback -- the real divergence is worse than that.
ui_bugs.selector: top-ribbon text / PDP delivery-info / cart free-delivery-progress-wrap

ui_bugs.issue: Cart and checkout summary show only 'Total £304.94 Including VAT' with no explicit 20% VAT line-item. Review request expected a separate VAT line. Probably acceptable for a VAT-inclusive storefront but worth confirming for legal/trade clarity.

'Congestion Charge (Central London) £15.00' is auto-added when postcode SW1A 1AA is entered but is not previewed anywhere before checkout (not in cart, not in PDP delivery copy). Sudden +£15 at payment may surprise/annoy London customers.
Variant label reads 'Variant: Please select' with orange warning even after size/finish are auto-populated. Add-to-Cart still works, but the UI looks like a validation error.

integration_issues.issue: Step 3 Payment does not render a Stripe Card Element so no card can be entered; 'Pay' button also fails its own validation due to address-state loss. Net effect: 0% of card orders can complete.
Going Step 1 -> Step 2 (fill) -> Step 3 -> click Pay re-renders Step 2 with blank address fields. Customer loses typed address every time they interact with payment.

issues: Free-delivery threshold inconsistent across three surfaces (£300 ribbon, £299 PDP, ~£1000 actual in cart progress bar)

verified_working: Homepage -> Marble-effect collection list -> PDP navigation
PDP renders image placeholder, H1, size/finish picker, volume pricing table, qty input, Add-to-Cart button, Samples button, Frequently Bought Together, Delivery info tabs
Add-to-Cart creates line item with correct qty 5 m² and line total £224.95 (= 5 × £44.99)

Cart page: item row, +/- qty, remove button (data-testid present), subtotal £224.95, delivery £79.99, total £304.94 Including VAT, promo-code field + Apply, Klarna & PayPal express-checkout buttons, Continue/Clear Basket, Delivery vs Collect-from-Store toggle (collect = FREE)
Cart persists across hard reload (same subtotal, same item count, same total)
Checkout Step 1 validates required fields and advances to Step 2 via [data-testid=checkout-to-step-2]
Checkout Step 2 exposes Home Delivery vs Click & Collect radio, Delivery Speed Standard/Express (+£25), Billing-same-as-delivery toggle, Order Notes textarea

Checkout Step 3 (when reached) renders payment-method radios for Card / PayPal / Klarna / Apple+Google Pay and Order Review with email + delivery address -- all selectors via data-testid (pay-method-card/paypal/klarna/wallet, place-order-btn)
Homepage ribbon reads £300 (matches backend default, NOT the £499 fallback in TileStationHome.js -- so the ribbon itself is fine in isolation)
action_items: P0: Mount Stripe Card Element on checkout Step 3. Verify <Elements stripe={stripePromise}> wraps the payment panel and <CardElement /> (or PaymentElement) is rendered unconditionally when payment-method=card. Confirm publishable key env var is loaded. Check that Stripe's inner iframe src contains 'elements-inner' in the DOM after Step 3 mounts.

P0: Fix checkout step-state persistence. When the user progresses to Step 3 and then the app re-renders Step 2 (either via back-nav or after a Pay-validation failure) the delivery address fields come back EMPTY. Persist the formState in the parent component / context / localStorage so Step 2 re-hydrates its inputs.

P0: Reconcile the free-delivery threshold across the three surfaces. Read ONE canonical value from backend settings and render the same figure in (a) homepage top ribbon, (b) PDP 'Delivery Information' box, (c) cart 'Spend £X more for FREE delivery' progress bar. Currently £300 / £299 / ~£1000 respectively.

P1: Gate 'Pay' button with clearer validation messaging -- right now the error 'Please fill in your delivery address' appears on Step 2 only after the user is already on Step 3 and hits Pay, which is confusing given the address WAS filled one step earlier.

P1: Verify end-to-end test payment with Stripe test card 4242... once card element renders, then confirm MongoDB shop_orders has order_number, delivery_method='collect' vs 'delivery', customer email, payment_status='paid'. Could not verify DB persistence in this pass because no order ever reached Stripe confirmation.
P2: Surface 'Congestion Charge £15 (Central London)' earlier (cart or PDP delivery info) instead of only at Step 3 payment.
P2: PDP 'Variant: Please select' warning shouldn't display once a size/finish is already chosen.
critical_code_review_comments: TileCheckoutPage likely does not keep checkout form state in a ref / context / persistent store -- Step 2 inputs lose their values on unmount. Refactor to store email/firstName/lastName/phone/addressLine1/city/postcode in a parent checkout context or in useReducer so every step reads/writes the same source of truth.

Stripe integration: confirm stripePromise is created once at module-scope with loadStripe(publishableKey), Elements provider wraps the Payment step, and the CardElement (or PaymentElement) is rendered synchronously -- do NOT gate it behind a setTimeout or a later effect that may never run. Also verify REACT_APP_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY (or equivalent) is populated in the pod env.

Free-delivery threshold should be a single backend setting returned by /api/settings (or /api/shop/config) and consumed by ribbon + PDP + cart. Having three different constants scattered across TileStationHome.js fallback / PDP component / cart progress component is the root cause.

Add a soft e2e smoke test (playwright) that exercises Homepage -> PDP -> Add-to-Cart -> Cart -> Checkout Step 1 -> Step 2 -> Step 3 -> card 4242 -> success page -> DB order_number assertion, and run it in CI before every deploy. Today's manual pass would have caught both P0s.

success_rate.frontend: Cart+PDP ~95% OK, Checkout Step 1 OK, Step 2 OK, Step 3 0% (card element missing + address state lost). Overall revenue path: FAIL.

test_credentials: Guest checkout (no login). Stripe TEST card 4242 4242 4242 4242 / 12/30 / 123 / SW1A 1AA -- never actually submitted because Card Element does not render.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes (1) Stripe Card Element mounting on Step 3, (2) checkout form-state persistence across steps, and (3) unifies the free-delivery threshold, re-run the same journey. Specifically assert: (a) after reaching Step 3, page.frames() contains an iframe whose URL matches /elements-inner/; (b) page.locator('input[name=cardnumber]') resolves inside that iframe; (c) after a successful test charge the browser lands on /shop/order-success (or equivalent) with a visible order number substring; (d) GET /api/shop/orders?order_number=XXX returns payment_status='paid' and delivery_method exactly matches what was selected (delivery|collect); (e) Spend-£X-more-for-FREE-delivery banner value equals the homepage ribbon value exactly.
rca of the issue: Two P0 root causes on the checkout page: (1) Stripe Card Element never mounts -- only Stripe's controller + metrics outer iframes are in the DOM; no elements-inner iframe. Likely the <Elements> provider isn't wrapping the Payment step, or CardElement is conditionally rendered on an event that doesn't fire, or the publishable key env var is missing. (2) Delivery address state is not preserved across step transitions -- reproduction: Step 1 fill+next, Step 2 fill address + Continue to Payment, Step 3 click Pay -> toast 'Please fill in your delivery address', view bounces back to Step 2 with empty fields. Root cause is most likely that each step component owns its own useState and the parent remounts the step on navigation instead of hoisting state. Mitigation: lift form state into a CheckoutContext / useReducer and optionally mirror to sessionStorage so a hard refresh also survives. Third issue (threshold mismatch) is a data-source fragmentation: the three surfaces read different constants/fallbacks; consolidate to one backend-driven value.
